metadata.title: Mycroft Project: The Volatility Surface Proposal
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Nik Bear Brown
metadata.credit_line: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. When an entire sector is priced for perfection, the appealing stocks are no longer on sale and offer little margin. The proposal adds Microstructure Intelligence Agents to read market signals below the level of headline prices. These agents calculate put/call ratios across multiple time horizons to detect shifting sentiment and risk. The stance is evidence-based and probabilistic: certainty is rare, so decisions rest on weighing the surface's signals.

artifactLines: When an entire sector is priced for perfection, the appealing stocks are no longer on sale and offer little margin.
The proposal adds Microstructure Intelligence Agents to read market signals below the level of headline prices.
These agents calculate put/call ratios across multiple time horizons to detect shifting sentiment and risk.
The stance is evidence-based and probabilistic: certainty is rare, so decisions rest on weighing the surface's signals.

beats.narration_text: The framing that a sector priced for perfection leaves nothing on sale, paired with adding Microstructure Intelligence Agents that compute put/call ratios across time horizons, is the mechanism I want to unpack. The line that certainty is rare fits an evidence-based approach. Can you (1) explain what 'priced for perfection' means in terms of an implied volatility surface, (2) show how put/call ratios across horizons would signal a mispriced sector, and (3) describe one concrete trade the microstructure agents would flag?
